commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 150s Codex dynamic-tool timeout for `skill_workshop` and a unit test for an apply call.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +17. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main has a 90s Codex dynamic-tool watchdog while the Skill Workshop lifecycle approval path can wait 120s plus 10s gateway grace. I did not run the live path because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair can preserve the intended approval fix while avoiding the broader default-timeout change.

target.title: fix(skill-workshop): bound lifecycle approval timeout below Codex dynamic-tool watchdog
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `skill_workshop` lifecycle tool calls (apply/reject/quarantine) invoked from a Codex agent session fail with "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms while running tool skill_workshop" before the operator approval prompt can resolve. The proposal is left pending and unmodified.
- **Root Cause**: The Codex dynamic-tool bridge wraps every tool call with a per-tool watchdog (`CODEX_DYNAMIC_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 90_000`, `dynamic-tool-execution.ts`). Skill Workshop lifecycle calls return a `requireApproval` payload that can wait up to `DEFAULT_PLUGIN_APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000` plus 10s gateway grace (130s total, `plugin-approvals.ts`, `agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`). The 90s watchdog fires first, surfacing a generic bridge timeout instead of a clean approval denial; the approval is never completed.
- **Fix**: Add `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 150_000` to `readConfiguredDynamicToolTimeoutMs`, matching the existing per-tool deadline pattern used for `message` and `image` tools. The 150s deadline exceeds the 130s approval ceiling, so the approval can now complete or expire cleanly before the Codex watchdog fires. Per-call `timeoutMs` overrides still take precedence. Non-Codex Skill Workshop callers are unaffected.
- **What changed**:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.ts` ‚ÄĒ exported `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 150_000` constant; added `skill_workshop` branch in `readConfiguredDynamicToolTimeoutMs`.
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts` ‚ÄĒ added test asserting `resolveDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs` returns `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS` for `skill_workshop`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Skill Workshop approval policy (`src/skills/workshop/policy.ts`) is unchanged; the 120s approval window is preserved for all callers.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/api.ts`, registry/loader).
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure Skill Workshop with default `approvalPolicy: pending`.
2. Start a Codex agent session and prompt it to apply a pending skill proposal.
3. The agent invokes `skill_workshop(action="apply", proposal_id="...")`.
4. **Before this PR**: the Codex dynamic-tool watchdog fires at 90s with "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms while running tool skill_workshop"; the approval prompt is never shown; the proposal stays pending.
5. **After this PR**: the 150s Codex deadline lets the approval flow reach its natural 120s expiry; if the operator does not respond, the approval times out with a clean denial; if the operator responds, the apply proceeds normally.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#91266)**: `skill_workshop(action="apply")` from a Codex agent session no longer surfaces a generic 90s dynamic-tool timeout; the approval path has sufficient time to resolve.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22 ‚ÄĒ Vitest against the production `resolveDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs` and `readConfiguredDynamicToolTimeoutMs` functions)**: `resolveDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs` called with `tool: "skill_workshop"` and no per-call `timeoutMs` override, verifying the returned value matches the exported constant.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:

```text
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

New test: "uses an extended deadline for skill_workshop lifecycle tool calls" ‚ÄĒ asserts `resolveDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs({ tool: "skill_workshop" })` equals `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS` (150,000ms).

**Observed result after fix**: `resolveDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs` returns 150,000ms for `skill_workshop` calls; per-call override and hard-cap paths are unchanged; the `message` and `image` sibling cases continue to return their existing constants.

**What was not tested**: live Gateway + Codex agent session with an active operator approval prompt; the approval path itself is covered by existing `agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts` tests which remain unchanged.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: `skill_workshop` non-lifecycle calls (list, inspect) also receive the 150s Codex deadline instead of the 90s default. **Mitigation**: non-lifecycle calls complete in milliseconds and are not affected by the wider window; per-call `timeoutMs` can still narrow this for any call.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Agent runtime
- [x] Codex extension

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #91266
target.author: openperf

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
